plan_metadata.name: Tab Local Debug Env Local Creation

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.

Type the text 'g#ed!-k?/h' into the "Application Name" input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code; note that an error message appears below the field indicating invalid input due to character and formatting restrictions.
@assertion there's message tell the app name is not available.

Click the "Back" button (with shortcut Ctrl+Alt+←) in the "Application Name" popup dialog at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, positioned within the red validation message area.
Click the "Default folder /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects" option in the workspace folder selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.

Type the text 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa' into the "Input an application name" field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, triggering a red error message indicating the app name exceeds 30 characters.
@assertion "app name is longer than the 30 characters" exist.

Click on the "Application Name" text input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code, where a validation error ("App name is longer than the 30 characters.") is shown due to the entered app name exceeding the character limit.

Click the "Default folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select `/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects` as the project root location in Visual Studio Code while setting up a Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit project.
Click the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.

Perform a right-click on the ".env.local" file within the "env" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code. The interaction point is marked precisely at the file name, highlighted by a red crosshair.
Click the "Delete" option in the context menu for the selected file ".env.local" in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Google Chrome browser window to close the Teams app tab.
Click the "Run and Debug" icon in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open debugging options. The interaction point is visually highlighted with a red crosshair on the icon.
